div.p: Calling backward() twice without zero_grad() causes gradient accumulation

ul.li: += accumulates: Must reset before each backward pass

div.p: ✅ Result: Correct gradient each epoch Each epoch uses fresh gradient from current loss
❌ Result: Exploding gradients Gradient grows: 0.5 → 0.9 → 1.2 → 1.5 → ... Training diverges!
The += is correct for multi-path gradients within a single backward pass, but causes incorrect accumulation across epochs if not reset.
Epoch 0: loss = 0.500 Epoch 100: loss = 0.150 Epoch 200: loss = 0.050 Epoch 300: loss = 0.010 Epoch 400: loss = 0.001 ✓
Epoch 0: loss = 0.500 Epoch 100: loss = 0.800 ⚠️ Epoch 200: loss = 5.200 ⚠️ Epoch 300: loss = 142.0 💥 Epoch 400: loss = NaN 💥
Update optimizer.step() param.data -= lr × 0.9 WRONG! (should be 0.4)
Epoch 2 (no zero) grad = 0.5 (stale) backward() grad += 0.4 grad = 0.9 ❌
Epoch 2 (with zero) zero_grad() grad = 0.0 backward() grad += 0.4 grad = 0.4 ✓
